lesson.menu_title: Pronunciation Lab II — Word Stress & Connected Speech
lesson.menu_desc: Stress the words that matter, soften the small ones, and let your speech flow — sound natural, not robotic — 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 — Pronunciation Lab II: Word Stress & Connected Speech
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 10 -- Pronunciation Lab II

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Last time we worked on single sounds. Today, three quick tips about rhythm, for your big presentation on Friday. First, stress the important words. In a sentence, the nouns, the main verbs, and the adjectives carry the meaning. Say them a little louder and longer. The small words, like a, the, to, and of, stay short and soft. Second, use contractions. Say I am as I'm, do not as don't, and we will as we'll. Contractions sound natural and friendly, not lazy. Third, let your words flow together. Do not stop after every word like a robot. Link the end of one word to the start of the next, so turn it off sounds like one smooth phrase. You do not need to speak fast. You need a clear, natural rhythm. Practice these three things for ten minutes a day, and on Friday you will sound calm and confident. You can do it. Talk soon.

Hey Daniela, it is Bruno from the Lisbon office. I heard you are working on your rhythm, so let me tell you my story. For a long time, I said every word with the same strong force. I love this project came out as I. Love. This. Project. It sounded like a robot, and people had to listen very hard. Then a colleague gave me one simple tip. Relax the small words, and push the big ones. So I started to say the important words clearly and let the little words almost disappear. I also began to use contractions, like I'm and we're, instead of the full forms. Suddenly people said my English sounded much more natural. This week I am working on linking, joining words together so they flow. By the way, your English already has a nice rhythm. Keep going, and I will see you at the team meeting on Thursday.
